However, doing that has not fixed the issue this time. Please advise.

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of SAP Central Business Configuration for Cloud Solutions through Scoping
entry.content:
Hello,

I would have 2 questions please:

1. Is there an overview available where I can see the list of Scope Items and their contents, like: GL Accounts, Reports, Account Groups, Fiori Apps etc? So basically a list of all Scope Items and their related content. I could not find such a file until now respectively an overview somewhere.

 

2. Some Scope Items mentioned in the YCOA COA File for Finance, column 8, cannot be found in the Availability Dependencies List. Why is that?

For example: J74 or J76 in the YCOA column N does not appear in the Availabilities Dependencies List. Where can I find a list of all possible Scope Items across all LoBs and Industries, in form of an Excel file?

 

Thank you!

Daniel Nicula

entry.title: Re: Improving my AdT Report template - part 3
entry.content:
Somtimes I want a very SIMPLE report template; it's so view lines, it's noch even an own blog:

CLASS lcl_report DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: main.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_report IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.
    "...code here:
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  NEW lcl_report( )->main( ).

entry.title: Re: Promotion Functionality in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content:
1.  You can have different publishing dates per country but you need to have custom fields configured in the template and then create the publishing instructions in the XML.

2.  There is not a standard report that shows who has been promoted but you can create an ad-hoc report based on the compensation template to extract the information you need

3.  Creating a different budget is handled just like any other budget.   You need a field to store the allocation (standard or custom) and then another field to store the spend (usually the promotion field)

4  Yes, you can use the extra (Adjustment) field in addition and configure the label as needed.   It is not connected to the promotion functionality

5.  I am not sure what you are trying to do in this question, however, before the promotion the now position needs to exist in the system.  In addition, if the new position will have direct reports, you are not going to be able to complete transfers so that the new employees report to the new manager in this transaction.  This needs to happen outside the compensation template in a separate activity.

David Somelofske

Principal Business Process Consultant

entry.title: Re: IAS ERROR READ & REASYNC JOB
entry.content:
Hi Yogananda! Thank you for your input.

I've reviewed the properties of both the source and destination systems, but certificate authentication has been implemented in both cases.

I'm sharing supporting images.

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content:
Thanks for the clarification. Your assumption might not be correct. Client side can only connect with the remote source by the usage of HTTP APIs, and in that sense CORS or Cross-Site Scripting could be an issue (which could happen in the cloud but on on premise). If that is the case I'd expect the OpenText solution has some sort of Admin/configuration panel to address that.

I saw something like this in 3051989 - "This content cannot be displayed in a frame" error if Business Workspace is opened from SAP for OpenText Extended ECM - SAP for Me) but I wonder if this is part of the same solution you are using (it is under component XX-PART-OPT-ECM-CLD)

To solve this problem:

  1. open Content Server admin page
  2. go to "Configure Security Parameters"
  3. unselect the option: "Frame Embedding: Prevent request handlers from being embedded in external frames"

There are different means by exposing an app in WorkZone. The app could be deployed in BTP or in the on-premise server and exposed via Content Federation (aka Content Exposure). WorkZone uses an iframe to embed some apps, which could also indicate a reason for not reaching the server.

That iFrame is the reason some HTTPs calls when done in SAPUI5 do not work in WorkZone unless you adapt the source code a little bit - but this is not always the case.

The easier way of understanding how this communication works, I highly suggest you doing it is my tracing the HTTP calls while you use the Fiori app in the browser by using the browser Developer Tools (any Fiori/front end developer can help you to do this in case you need support). In Chrome or Edge, just press F12 while having the app opened an check the "Network" tab.

I suggest to repeat this process both when executing the app in the local network and in work zone. If the call in workzone really fails, it should be even easier to identify in the Network tab as it will return some 4xx or 5xx error (despite being read).

Please attach those snapshots here. You can check the Headers >> General section for that request to see the URL being called and compare both scenarios.

The "Console" tab is also useful in case of CORS, Cross-Site Scripting issues.

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice workflow
entry.content: Hi Fernanda, all links are either obsolete or we as partners are not authorized. Can you please update them?

entry.title: Access to Management Bank – Cash Management Application
entry.content:
Which Scope item is required to access the Management Bank – Cash Management application.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Access to Management Bank – Cash Management Application
entry.content: Scope item BFA "Basic Bank Account Management" will be required for the App Manage Bank - Cash Management. https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolP/BFA

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If in step 4.6.2 Perform Manual Reconciliation you only see one bank account with status "Not Applicable", it usually means:

What likely went wrong :

  • No open items to reconcile – The bank statement may have already cleared everything automatically.
  • No bank statement uploaded or processed – Manual reconciliation needs posted statement items.
  • Bank GL account not set as open item managed – Reconciliation requires this setting.
  • Wrong filters – Could be using the wrong company code or account filter.

What to check :

  • Use Manage Bank Statements to confirm a statement was uploaded and items are not cleared.
  • Use Display Journal Entries to see if postings hit the bank GL.
  • Use Manage G/L Account Master Data to check that the GL is open item managed.
  • Review filters in Perform Manual Reconciliation.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Issue with User Visibility in People Picker on Manage Flexible Workflow Application
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of configuring a workflow within the Manage Flexible Workflow application. However, we are encountering an issue where no users are visible in the People Picker field. Despite ensuring that all required prerequisites as per the KBA mentioned below have been met, we are still unable to see any users listed.

0002824240 - No users available in People picker of Manage workflow application

0002869055 - No users available in Manage Workflows app - SAP S/4HANA

We have followed the standard setup guidelines and checked the user assignments, but the problem persists. Could anyone from the community suggest what could be causing this issue or any troubleshooting steps we might have missed?

entry.title: Goods Movement Analysis : Consumption Quantity wrong
entry.content:
Hi 

I'm looking for into  Goods Movement Analysis and I don't understand why SAP does not considered as Consumption Quantity the movement 601 with Special Stock Type = E.

They come from MTO orders . 

Example 

Material   Mov      Stock type   Qty

AAAAA     601                             10

AAAAA     601               E            5

In my opinion the total consumption must be 15 and not 10 ( ad per SAP)

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Ensure License Compliance
entry.content:
Hi Jacek,

I don't know what happened but somehow the Consumption node and Show Report action started to show again after Reset. I added the consumption authority to the backofficeadministrator role which is a member of the admingroup. My has admingroup role but it was not working until I assign the backofficeadministrator role. It looks like authority are not searched recursively through roles.

Pascal

entry.title: Re: Responsible Cost Center in WBS element and Project
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

this is a common point of confusion in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition regarding the role of the Responsible Cost Center on WBS Elements/Projects and settlement strategy configuration.

 

1. Responsible Cost Center in Project/WBS

  • The Responsible Cost Center is mandatory because it determines who “owns” or manages the project/WBS from a controlling perspective.
  • This cost center controls where costs are posted and also drives the company code determination for the project, since cost centers are assigned to a controlling area and company code.
  • It helps ensure proper cost assignment and internal controlling, for example in cost planning, budget monitoring, and settlements.

 

2. Company Code Determination from Cost Center

  • The system derives the Company Code for the project based on the company code assigned to the responsible cost center.
  • This is standard behavior, because cost centers belong to a controlling area, which belongs to a company code, and projects must be assigned to exactly one company code.

 

3. Settlement Strategy & Settlement Rule in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot manually create or change settlement strategies like in SAP ECC or Private Cloud versions.
  • The settlement strategy is predefined by SAP standard for projects and WBS elements.
  • You do not select settlement strategies manually; instead, the system automatically uses the default settlement rule created during WBS or project creation.

 

4. Where is the Settlement Strategy Managed in Public Cloud?

  • The settlement rules and strategies are typically predefined and managed by SAP.
  • The Responsible Cost Center in WBS drives automatic creation of a settlement receiver in the settlement rule, generally pointing to the cost center or the internal order assigned.
  • You can review settlement rules in the project system, but modifications are limited or restricted in Public Cloud.
  • In some cases, you can configure settlement profiles or parameters via SAP Best Practice Configuration in the Manage Your Solution app or via SAP Fiori apps, but direct creation of settlement strategies as in ECC is not possible.
  • CBC (Central Business Configuration) manages broad scope items and business configuration but does not allow creating custom settlement strategies for projects.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite Error
entry.content: Tried the above and still cannot create an Integration Flow Artifact within the Package

entry.title: Re: Max retry and duration of retry by CIG / Spend Manage...
entry.content:
CIG will try to resend the ddocument failed, 3 times (once per hour) afte the initial failure.
In any case, when the document failed and has enter "RETRY" or "FAILED", the CIG Admin can come back and select all the failed transaction and click on "Reprocess >> Oubound" that will resend all the documents again.
Note that CIG keeps the transaction of the last 30 days.

entry.title: Re: My BTP Trail account Subscription failed for Integrat...
entry.content:
I had this occur for me also today.  Delete the failed Integration Suite installation and try again.  Also make sure that your login has not expired.  I found that I had timed out of my trial account and had to login again as well.  After that, it successfully installed the Integration Suite.

I did the re-install of Integration Suite because when going to Design -> Integrations and APIs -> Package, I still cannot add and Integration Flow to the Package.  This is still not solved and a number of people have reported this and are stuck.  Hoping SAP will look at their CPI Trial Account and figure out what is causing this problems, so we can continue learning about Integration Flows.

entry.title: Re: Moving average Price History in s4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. After talking to the experts, I can provide the following answer: A price history for all materials in a single report is not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition at the moment. You may request features on the Customer Influence Portal. We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback. Kind regards, Jessica

entry.title: SAP Solution Manager ChaRM how to fill Sold-To Party automatically
entry.content:
Dear 

I tried the KBA 2612173 for my RFC documents.

#######

  1. Execute Transaction code IB52.
  2. Input Installed IBase 1 and hit "enter" key on keyboard.
  3. Select the system from the tree structure on left hand side.
  4. Click 'goto' on top menu.
  5. Select 'partner'.
  6. Maintain Sold-To party data in the pop-up, and save.

#########

But "Sold-to party" is not filled automatically when creating an RFC document. Please advise.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manager ChaRM how to fill Sold-To Party...
entry.content:
KBA 2612173 outlines that the steps you followed only work for ITSM documents (Incidents, etc.) where there is an IBase field in the standard Transaction type (e.g. SMIN). If there is no IBase field as standard in the Transaction type, then the "Sold-to party" value cannot be determined automatically. 

The standard ChaRM Change Documents do not include an IBase field, so the method used by the ITSM documents will not work out-of-the-box. If you want this functionality, the KBA outlines that there are 2 options you can take:

  1. Create template RFC documents where the "Sold-to party" is filled while creating the template. Then any RFCs created from the template will already have the relevant "Sold-to party" value.
    • This option requires no Customization, and will work out of the box. But it is not dynamic, the values will be fixed in the templates. Users will need to know the correct template to use when creating their Change Documents.
  2. Read the Support Team Determination via Business Rule Framework plus documentation and customize your Z Change Document Transaction types so that they make use of custom BRF+ determination rules.
    • This option requires Customization, and is not considered standard functionality of the Change Documents. However, it can be dynamic and respond to other data in the Change Document (depending on your BRF+ determination rules). This is quite complex Customization, but can be very powerful if done right.

Any idea what the problem could be or how we can solve it?

ATC from the BTP to the on premise system works.

entry.title: CAP + Fiori Elements - Structure of app
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am pretty new to all the cloud development topics and have been assigned a project where I have to migrate a Z-Cockpit to the cloud as a Side-By-Side Extension. I have been trying around a little and would like to clear up some doubts I have in terms of viability or to double-check if might not be going in a completely wrong direction.

Status Quo:

I have build a FE app using the list report template and connected it to a CAP Backend (OData V4) which is consuming an OData-Service (V2) from a S4HC system. This OData-service is exposing some CBOs as well as some finance related data associated to the main entity. 

Requirement:

On the FE's object page there is a table displaying a sub-entity to the main entity. The user needs to enter data somewhere so that per trigger action (table action?) I can use the input data as well as the initial data of the sub entity to A create a finance document (SAP Standard) from my cap backend and B update the sub entity (CBO) on the S4HC system. The input data is necessary for the FI document creation.

My approach

1. I was considering declaring a new associated sub entity to the sub entity (so a sub-sub entity) to make it appear in the table as well and then editable - so that the user can enter data - on custom action button trigger my cap action and pass the values to the CAP action that I need.

2. On click on one of the rows of the table I navigate to a custom page where I create some custom entry fields - let the user enter data - and on custom button click trigger the CAP action and pass the values to the CAP action (Not sure if the data of the sub entity's row can be acessed here?)

I would appreciate it a lot if you could guide me here and give me some tips whether I am taking the right approach here or if this sounds rather like a SAPUI5 Freestyle case to you and if it indeed a viable case for FE which of 1 or 2 would be preferable or even suggest a better approach to me.

Big thanks in advance and regards,

Oliver

entry.title: Round business rule always going to 0
entry.content:
I created a business rule to round a number with the precision of 2 and a null threshold. My expectation is if the number contains up to two decimal points in the value, it should continue to show the two decimals. However, all values are coming back with 0 value after the decimal point.

For example: 100,565.32 is updated to 100,565.00 when this rule is in place.

 

What am I missing?

Here is part of my package json :

"cds": {
"i18n": {
"folders": [
"i18n",
"_i18n",
"/node_modules/my-i18ncommon-content/src/i18n"
]
 
At runtime the translation is properly made.
 
Thanks in advance for your support !
 
},

entry.title: Re: EDI - Sales Orders
entry.content:
You have mentioned - If there are any issues e.g. incorrect Material number, the EDI Order stays at the Middleware company. 

How is the Middleware company able to validate the Master Data of receiving system? 

As mentioned earlier, normally we receive all the EDI orders from Middleware and those with incorrect details end up in error. The Users then review each one with error and undertakes necessary corrective measures.

entry.title: Re: SAP build work zone, Custom Visualization of Tile is...
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-tile-visualization-amp-navigation-in-sap-build-workzone-cloud/ba-p/14087171

entry.title: Re: Equipment Loan in SAP S4 Service
entry.content:
Hi,

Below is an outline of the typical steps involved in the Equipment Loaner process:

1. Create Loaner Order

Initiate a loaner order for the equipment that needs to be lent out. This includes specifying the customer details, equipment information, and conditions of the loan.

2. Equipment Dispatch

Once the loaner order is created, arrange for dispatch of the equipment to the customer’s location. This may involve logistics planning and coordination.

3. Equipment Receipt

Upon receipt of the equipment, confirm its condition and ensure all details are recorded accurately in the system.

4. Equipment Usage

Monitor the usage of the equipment while it is with the customer. Keep track of any maintenance or issues reported by the customer.

5. Equipment Return

Ensure the equipment is returned according to the agreed terms. Conduct a post-return inspection to assess the condition of the equipment upon its return.

6. Documentation and Billing

Adjust the documentation as necessary and process any billing related to the loan. This could include rental fees or costs for any damages.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Problems Posting Through FBV0/FBVB with Custom BKPF Fields - msg 349

entry.title: Re: Split the dunning by type of correspondence
entry.content:
Hi Ronny,

Thanks for your reply.

Tried to use the preselections for Business Partner as suggested, but it seems that the views generated take in consideration BUT000/ADRC joining with tables BUT020/BUT021_FS

ending up to filter wrongly the data. Do you know if there is any way to change this default selections when creating the preselections?

It would be good if you add that into github project or share repo for others to understand the structure and code syntax .. please prepare and add that in your blog would be beneficial..

entry.title: Re: Understanding Joule - (Step #7) Joule for Developers
entry.content:
@Raja_Gupta - I want to begin using Joule for Developers but not for a scenario specifically listed above.  For example, I have an on-premise S/4HANA 1709 system connected to BTP via Cloud Connector.   I also own SAP BTP ABAP Cloud (64GB) where I've run the Fiori Custom Code App previously.   Lastly, I have a BTP EA with SAP AI Units and I own the licenses mentioned in SAP Note 3571857.  

Can I use the ABAP Explain and ABAP AI capabilities within ADT against my on-premise S/4HANA 1709 system? I know I can generate ABAP in BTP ABAP Cloud and S/4HANA Cloud 2052+.   But what about ABAP AI features for on-premise?  

Great blog above - Thank you in advance!

Is there a solution to this? Thanks.

secondly, the line displaying the total of all Fundcenter/commitement items do not appear as in the standard report

finally when i double click on the yellow line of my custom report to get the details of consumption, it take me firt to the standard report, before i double click again to get the result.

Could you please help

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Practice-systems/basic-abap-programming NOT FOUND (404)
entry.content: please bring back the systems, can someone, somewhere help???

entry.title: Request to Delete Bank Master Data.
entry.content:
How to delete bank master data in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request to Delete Bank Master Data.
entry.content:
1. Use archiving object FI_BANKS to archive the marked for deletion banks. Refer to: Archiving Banks Using FI_BANKS
  2. Ensure the bank is marked for deletion from the App Manage Bank - Master Data.
  3. Confirm the bank is not used anymore.
  4. Follow the ILM-Related Information for the Archiving Object. Refer to: ILM-Related Information for the Archiving Object
  5. Define the archiving variant containing the parameters for the banks to be archived in the App Manage Archiving Variants.
  6. The Marked for Deletion indicator can also updated API Bank. Refer to: Bank and Entity
  7. Delete bank master data records from BNKA table by updating the relevant entity with the IsMarkedForDeletion indicator. After updating the entity, delete it with an archiving job if the record is not used anywhere.

entry.title: Re: While confirming a Transfer order in LT12 i get the f...
entry.content:
Hi Dominik ,

After implementing the now this error doesnt occur that frequent , but however it comes up now and then ,yesterday as well when the user was confirming the transfer order he was getting an Error that there is 0 quantity of the given material.

However there 1 pc quantiity available in the storage area.

Anything else that can be checked ....

entry.title: Error SuccessFactors SSO test: Authentication Failed: This typically indicates BizX SAML settings...
entry.content:
Dear Sap community,

Whilst testing SSO for SuccessFactors via upgrade center, I ran in to following error:


Authentication Failed: This typically indicates BizX SAML settings contains an invalid certificate.

Is there anyone who has exprienced the same issue and can point me in the right direction please?

Kind regards
G

entry.title: Re: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
Hi  @Andrey_M Thanks for the detailed blog on APRS. I have few questions on the BDC integration and HDLF as the target.

1. Does APRS only configured and used for SAP managed data products? or can this be used in Custom Data products from Z CDS views in S/4 HANA to Datasphere object store?

2. HDLF as the target mentioned in this blog,  is not the one in Datasphere HDLF right?

3. In this SAP managed data products integration using APRS, Datasphere replication flows are not used right?

4. When Intelligent/Insight app is activated, the APRS initial and delta process is initiated from S/4 and load the data into HDLF of BDC Foundation service, then why an integration Datasphere space is created with replication flows to load the data for these data products? I am quoting SAP help, https://help.sap.com/docs/business-data-cloud/administering-sap-business-data-cloud/install-intelligent-applications

entry.title: Change of feeder class name in SAP MDG for BP
entry.content:
HI Experts,

I have a small doubt as below and suggest me the same. Thanks.

1. We have a custom uibb where we kept the custom feeder class

2. For checking standard behaviour, we replaced the custom feeder class to standard feeder class

3. Again, we changed the standard feeder class to custom feeder class

post that , New button was not working. When we click on the NEW button system hangs .

I checked the feeder class parameters/setting.

Is there any other settings do i need to carry?

Kindly suggest me the same. thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP spool generated by smartforms restricted with 1000 pages
entry.content: You may contact SAP Support to analyze the problem.

entry.title: dunning notice generated but spool is in error
entry.content:
Synopsis

Dunning parameters entered

dunning scheduled done

1dunning notice generated of which 1 to be sent

dunning print out done with LP01 

Spool with pdf tried to open but error "Unable to call Corefunction callREADFILE:SY-SUBRC=16

entry.title: The Heart of Transformation: People, Process, and Persistence
entry.content:
(Blog 1 of the “Transformation in Field Service” series)

Introduction

When we hear the word transformation, our minds often jump to technology—shiny new tools, artificial intelligence, mobile apps, cloud platforms. But in my experience working in Field Service—and as a parent, gardener, and lifelong learner—I’ve found that successful transformation isn’t just digital.

It’s deeply human.

The Family Trip That Almost Didn’t Happen

A few weekends ago, we planned a “short” hike as a family. With a three-year-old and a four-year-old in tow, I expected some negotiation. One wanted to stay and build a fort in the living room. The other had already left the house wielding a stick like an explorer on a mission. My partner and I were somewhere in the middle—juggling snacks, sunhats, and our sanity.

What made the hike possible wasn’t the gear or the route. It was alignment, motivation, and compromise. We adjusted expectations. Built excitement. Let the kids help plan the snack break. And eventually, we all made it to the top—tired, proud, and a little muddy.

Honestly? That’s what transformation looks like.

What It Means for Field Service

The same lessons apply when transforming service operations. You might be rolling out new scheduling software or launching mobile tools for technicians. On paper, the plan looks perfect. But in reality, adoption lags. Resistance creeps in.

Why? Because technology alone doesn’t change behavior.

Successful transformation requires:

  • People who feel involved, heard, and capable

  • Processes that are redesigned—not just digitized

  • Persistence to navigate detours, doubts, and delays

📘 Want a model? Try Kotter’s 8-Step Change Model

Dr. John Kotter’s framework is widely used in business transformation. It emphasizes:

  1. Creating urgency

  2. Building a guiding coalition

  3. Forming a strategic vision

  4. Enabling action by removing barriers

  5. Celebrating short-term wins

  6. And anchoring the changes in culture

Explore the full model →

Technology Is the Enabler, Not the Driver

Tech is powerful. But it’s like giving my kids high-end hiking gear. If they don’t know where they’re going—or why—the gear ends up in the mud.

In Field Service, this means:

  • Involving technicians when introducing mobile apps

  • Listening to dispatchers when reimagining cloud-based workflows

  • Aligning leadership KPIs to real-world service outcomes—not just feature checklists

🧠 Need a practical tool? Consider ADKAR

I have seen companies successfully applying the ADKAR model from Prosci that helps manage individual transitions across 5 stages:

  • Awareness of the need for change

  • Desire to participate in the change

  • Knowledge of how to change

  • Ability to implement change

  • Reinforcement to sustain change

More on ADKAR from Prosci →

What’s Next in This Series

This is the first post in a five-part series exploring key transformations in Field Service:

  1. Digital transformation to mobile for field technicians

  2. Embracing AI in service operations

  3. Migrating from on-premise solutions to cloud dispatching

  4. Transitioning to S/4HANA

  5. And throughout: putting people, process, and persistence at the center

🌱 Book tip: Switch: How to Change Things When Change Is Hard

A highly readable book by Chip & Dan Heath, which uses the metaphor of the rider, the elephant, and the path. In Field Service, you often need to steer minds (rider), motivate hearts (elephant), and shape the environment (path).

More on Switch here →

entry.title: Re: Ensure License Compliance
entry.content:
Hi @phoude,

I'm happy to hear that everything works now. You are right - authorities comes from a single role. If you have multiple roles assigned (directly and/or transitionally), you need to select one that you act upon. Then only authorities from this role are taken into consideration.

Cheers,

Jacek

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hello @nageshcaparthy 

thank you for your setup guide. Helps a lot. 

I have performed the setup process. But Joule is unable to retrieve data from my S/4 System e.g. purchase orders or sales orders. I have appropriate business roles assigned to my user. Also it's not able to link me to an Fiori app. When I'm asking Joule if it is able to retrieve data, it's answering: 

"I currently do not have the capability to retrieve data directly from your S/4 system, including the number of Sales Orders created. For this information, you may need to access your system directly or consult with your IT department or system administrator who can provide you with the necessary reports or data."

What am I missing?

Thanks and regards
Tung

entry.title: Re: how to send Quality Notification to supplier Via emai...
entry.content:
Hi Kalidas_Lande,

Could kindly explain the logic you currently have in the OPD and let us know what is not working for you?

Here are some help links:
Printing or Sending Quality Notifications

Output Management for Quality Notifications

Determining Email Recipients

For example, have you tried the different available values for the email address?

Please also check the BAdI extensibility options

For example: 

Set Default Values and Default Partners When Creating a NotificationBADI_QQM_NOTIF_DEFAULT_VAL_CLD

If you can let us know what you tried, someone here can maybe help determine the next steps

thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you please advise on how to obtain metadata details for a CDS view entity? The DD03L table stores metadata for classic CDS views, which have an SQL view name. However, even if I add the technical view name for the entity, it still does not appear in the DD03L table. Is there another table that contains metadata information for CDS view entities? 

Please advise

entry.title: [Webinar] Seamlessly Integrate Service Now with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Agenda :

  • Introduction to ServiceNow and SAP Cloud ALM 
  • Integration Framework – APIs and data mapping   
  • Use Cases and Applications 
  • Step-by-Step Integration Process 

Register Here : https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/2cf96277-b3ef-4e1b-9370-cda33f224550@42f7676c-f455-423c-82f6-dc2d99791af7

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
Thanks @jeetendrakapase ,   I have a follow up question on this note that you answered. 

Is the SAP-managed Space within SAP Datasphere separate from the Foundation Services? Are there only the activated objects of i.e. Insight Apps inside that space? YES, its an ingestion space for Datasphere.

If the foundation service is different from the SAP managed space of Datasphere, What is the main purpose of Datasphere ingestion space when an intelligent/insight app is activated?  I see that local tables and replication flows are activated in this space, that means even though data gets to Foundation service HDLF, again that needs to be transferred to Datasphere Ingestion space local tables?

entry.title: ABAP Practice System Gone - when is it coming back
entry.content: we were using the ABAP Practice System and then it was gone, when is it coming back?

entry.title: [Webinar] Streamlining automation Testing: Integrating SAP Cloud ALM with S/4HANA TAT & TTA
entry.content:
Agenda :

  • Introduction to SAP Cloud ALM 
  • Configure S/4 HANA Test Automation Tools (TAT) for Public Cloud   
  • Enable Tricentis Test automation (TTA) for Private Cloud  
  • Walk through the use cases and demo 

Register Here : https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/23b9e6e4-590f-4de9-86e9-00fc9aca70a2@42f7676c-f455-423c-82f6-dc2d99791af7

entry.title: Re: IAS ERROR READ & REASYNC JOB
entry.content:
Hi

I suggest to check in the "outbound certificates" section of the IAS target system.

The certificate there should match with a user defined as "Systems" in the Administrators of IAS

If the user you're updating is an IAS administrator, the "System user" requires additional permissions. With the default confing I had the issue that IAS admins could not be updated by the job because of lack of permissions of the "System" user linked to the certificate

entry.title: Re: Can you tell me the view where I can check the BP Mas...
entry.content:
Hi db11

You can check the View Browser app - with that app you can search for views or even views that contain a certain field.

You can also search in the Help CDS View section: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/5418de55938d1d22e10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

For the Credit Risk Class, I_BusinessPartnerCreditProfile contains the risk class, but it is not a release view at this time.

However, I_CreditManagementBP seems to be released and has the field. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/aa0e12fdc63c4992afd7e8335b972d99.html?locale=en-US

For the industry:

Please check I_BusinessPartner and see if this is the one you are looking for

Or I_BUSPARTINDUSTRYCODE or I_BuPaIdentification

Please let us know how it turns out

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: [Webinar] Change and Deployment Management with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Agenda : 

  • Learn how SAP Cloud ALM supports a clean core strategy by enabling structured and efficient change processes.
  • Explore deployment capabilities, from transport management to orchestration of changes across landscapes.
  • See how SAP Cloud ALM structures the entire change lifecycle from planning to go-live with full transparency and traceability.
  • Understand which SAP solutions and system types are currently supported and how this continues to evolve.
  • Watch SAP Cloud ALM in action with a real-world walkthrough of change and deployment scenarios.
  • Get a sneak peek into upcoming features and the strategic direction of change and deployment in SAP Cloud ALM.

Register Here : https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/79d626c3-8b28-41ac-893d-cc2fcbe3a2c5@42f7676c-f455-423c-82f6-dc2d99791af7

entry.title: Looking for CDS view based extractors that have replaced the classical extractors.
entry.content:
Hi All,

In my current setup based on SAP ECC, I am using the below 5 extractors. I would like to know if SAP has replaced these classical extractors with CDS view based extractors in S4HANA. Can you please suggest the CDS views?

  1. 0FC_OP_01
  2. 0FC_CI_01
  3. 0FC_BP_ITEMS
  4. 0FC_PAY
  5. 0FC_DUN_ITEMS

Thanks & Regards,

Nageswara

entry.title: IW21 – It is allowing a PM notification to be opened with INAC status.
entry.content:
The system is not blocking the creation of PM notifications for equipment that is registered with the system statuses INAC and DLFL.

According to the standard documentation, it should not be possible to perform this type of activity.

entry.title: How to Identify Your SAP BTP Global Administrator in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Symptom: You don't know who the BTP Global Admin is.

Requirements: You need to have an S-user and the role of Super Administrator or Cloud Administrator assigned in SAP for Me. 

  • If you don't have an S-User, please check this page.
  • If you are unsure which role you have, please check this note. 

Resolution:

1) Open SAP for Me and log in.

2) Navigate to the "Dashboards" section and click "Systems & Provisioning".

3) Select "Systems".

4) Search for "Business Technology Platform".

5) Click the link provided in the Global Account Column.

6) A new page will open. Scroll down to find the "Global Account Administrators" card.

Image 1

Image 2

Source: 2669325 - How to add a user as a SAP Business Technology Platform Global Account administrator

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Multiple of the same Tax Type on single Invoice line

Kindly refer this thread for details on the topic - Multi VAT on single invoice line

entry.title: Connection tab keeps loading and fails "logon.faces"
entry.content:
I'm trying to open CMC on port 6400, and everything works as expected. However, when I try to open the "Connection" tab, it keeps loading and eventually fails. I've read that some people suggest opening port 6405 to fix this issue, but I’d like to get your feedback first.

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance - Roadmap Session at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
I recently discussed the topic of master data management in various sessions at Sapphire 2025, Orlando. For example, I discussed how to get more complete, accurate, and compliant enterprise data with a modern master data management system and what is MDM's role in data fabric, data product, and AI initiatives.

Fully packed session at Sapphire 2025

I also outlined  our plans for the SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) solution portfolio in a roadmap session:

Session title

The MDG roadmap session again saw a large crowd of attendees at this year's Sapphire and our growth and development plans for the MDG portfolio resonated well with the audience. But I know that not everybody can travel to Orlando, I hence would like to share the news with everybody who couldn't join. (The slides below are the ones that I used in the Sapphire roadmap session.)

Please note that the roadmap disclaimer is indicating that future items are subject to change applies.

Solution Introduction

To get started, let me very briefly explain what SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) is, and which topics it addresses.

SAP Master Data Governance at a glance

MDG is an application to manage master data, it provides preconfigured, domain-specific master data governance to monitor, find and correct data quality issues. You can either centrally create, change, and distribute, or you can consolidate master data coming from different systems in your entire enterprise landscape.

MDG is one application for all master data. It is not only for SAP master data. It does come with a lot of out-of-the-box, domain-specific capabilities that are, of course, based on SAP’s data models and business logic. But it is also a platform that is used by many customers to build master data governance processes on extended data models and completely custom-defined master data domains.

SAP Master Data Governance key capabilities

 From our customers, we understand that the purpose of master data management is to improve the quality of master data and to establish a corporate-wide understanding of it, enabling them to run and optimize their business based on a trusted master data foundation.

As you probably know, companies take different approaches to MDM. For example, some allow for de-central ownership of master data, and create a common understanding by using master data consolidation to create key mapping between detected duplicates and to create a golden record. Others prefer central ownership of master data and distribute master data that is always created in high quality. Some companies decide to take a de-central approach for some domains, like customer master data, and a central approach for other domains, for example, when introducing new products. On top of that, many companies constantly monitor how the quality of master data evolves. And then they react if they find issues.

MDG supports all these approaches and also all combinations of these approaches in a very integrated and organized way, and this is very much appreciated by our customers. This allows you to combine approaches as required to cover all use cases and all implementation styles, for a complete enterprise master data management program with just one solution: SAP MDG.

Strategy and Vision

Our strategy and vision for MDG have been very consistent for years, and we are successfully executing this vision. Our MDG product strategy is not an isolated strategy just for MDG - it is embedded into SAP’s broader strategy and vision, which is outlined in this slide.

SAP vision bringing together applications, data, and AI

Through SAP’s unparalleled applications, our end-to-end business processes connect all parts of an organization. This ensures processes are smooth, consistent, and ready for AI-driven improvements.

SAP can also provide unmatched data, because by harmonizing information from SAP and non-SAP sources into one unified Business Data Cloud, we give AI agents a 360-degree perspective, and everything is instantly ‘understood’ in its business context.

When you then bring the right data to specialized AI agents orchestrated by SAP Joule, you unlock continuous optimization across lines of business. Instead of isolated automation, you get an interconnected system that learns, adapts, and improves at scale. We think that this is the best approach to AI.

This synergy between applications, data, and AI is the driving force behind the SAP Business Suite. 

SAP MDG supporting the new era of enterprise management

The graphic above illustrates how MDG will support SAP’s broader vision of bringing together applications, data, and AI. Let me start from the bottom:

As pointed out before, SAP offers a suite of applications with end-to-end scenarios – and not just isolated applications. Accordingly, we have built MDG not just as a solution “next to” applications. Of course, MDG can be an MDM solution for any heterogeneous application landscape. But in the context of SAP applications, we can offer much more integration. We have shown this with scenarios embedded into SAP S/4HANA, or with federated MDG deployments across an application landscape, or with integrated scenarios across SAP Ariba Supplier and Lifecycle Performance and MDG.

When we look at SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), this provides semantically rich data from various data sources, and master data often is the most critical data asset. MDG curates this master data towards a trustful and high-quality treasure, really unlocking the value of other business data, or also feeding into 360 views via Intelligent Apps as well as data products within BDC.

When it comes to Artificial Intelligence and MDG there are two key aspects: on the one hand MDG leverages AI technology for process automation to speed up MDG processing. But more importantly, the trusted high-quality master data in MDG, provided to all applications and to SAP BDC, truly enables SAP Business AI across all SAP standard and custom-built AI scenarios and agents.

SAP Master Data Governance in SAP Business Suite

The SAP Business Suite represents the culmination of SAP’s overall vision for the future of enterprise management. It combines core ERP and Line of Business applications, and it is fueled by business data and meaningful AI. It is a comprehensive, integrated set of solutions designed to seamlessly connect and optimize every function across your enterprise. This solution architecture, as shown in the graphic above, ensures that all parts of your business operate in sync, unlocking agility, efficiency, and innovation.

In this context, you may ask yourself where SAP MDG fits in: SAP MDG is technically built on the SAP Business Technology Platform and commercially part of the BTP. And it is truly the enabler of the SAP Business Suite’s applications and of SAP Business Data Cloud by providing trusted and high-quality master data for flawless end-to-end business processes, trustful insights, and reliable automation through Business AI.

Investment drivers for SAP Master Data GovernanceHow does this translate into the strategic investments that we are planning for SAP MDG’s roadmap? Our roadmap for MDG is driven by the integrated vision and value proposition of the SAP Business Suite, and by customer needs and master data management market trends that arise in this context.

With the benefits of the SAP Business Suite at hand, SAP can provide much more value to our customers than just any MDM tool provider, because we understand how master data is used in business contexts. Leveraging this understanding, we create benefits for our customers. We can provide master data management capabilities that truly support all domains, all industries, and all approaches, embedded into the business. We have proven this with MDG, and we will continue to deliver on this unique promise. 

In addition, we see certain trends in the market, which will be laid out in in more detail further below. But let's start with highlighting how interrelated these trends are.

For example, new sources for trusted data in the cloud, or data sharing in business networks in the cloud will allow for more automation in keeping your master data records up to date.

Or take the fact that digitization or the application of artificial intelligence demands for higher volumes and better quality of master data, and therefore needs more automation in master data management.

As we know, the market for business application software is moving towards modular but integrated applications and towards the cloud. For most companies, this move to the cloud is not one single step. To facilitate a step-by-step journey, modularization is a key enabler that allows to also move MDM capabilities to the cloud step-by-step, as required by the applications.

Cloud as strategy driver

Let’s come to the first trend: the cloud. When business applications are moving to the cloud, the master data itself needs to be provided there. Of course, this is possible with MDG today. But in addition, we will continue to provide cloud-based master data integration services on the SAP Business Technology Platform. We will also build out data quality services as microservices in the cloud. We currently offer Address-Validation-as-a-Service against always up-to-date address directories, in the cloud.

I already mentioned that a stepwise move of business applications to the cloud demands for a modular approach to MDM. We are addressing this need with federation of MDG. This will, by the way, also help to meet the increasingly local compliance regulations. Modularization and cloud consumption will also enable our customers to get to a positive return on investment much faster and to grow their enterprise MDM program exactly in the pace needed for their strategic business initiatives.

Strategy drivers: digitization and AI

I think we all agree that master data is the key resource of any digital transformation and AI initiative. We will enhance MDG to help companies to become more intelligent, more agile, and to always process data compliantly.

Digitization and AI will demand for modular, specialized business applications, and we will help to provide harmonized, high-quality master data to these applications to enable end-to-end processes for intelligent enterprises. The key drivers we are focusing on in MDG are automation enablement, efficiency, and master data quality management. 

In the modular application landscape of the future, it will be a challenge to provide frictionless digital processes. SAP’s Business Technology Platform will be the foundation to achieve this. SAP Business Data Cloud for SAP and non-SAP integration will enable this. And this will provide our customers with a very high return on their investment into master data quality, because SAP’s common data foundation will allow to then broadly apply artificial intelligence as well as analytical scenarios to all data, and this is then powered by high-quality master data provided by MDG. This means that every investment in enhancing master data with MDG will yield significant returns in all your data-driven scenarios on top of SAP BTP and BDC. 

We also continue to see clear proof of this interrelation of digitization and investments into master data management, as many of the current SAP S/4HANA transformation programs at our customers use MDG to prepare this fundamental move for their company.

Strategy driver: automation

As pointed out before, digitization and AI are key drivers for automation in MDM. We will leverage SAP’s deep knowledge in business applications, analytics, and business networks to reduce the need for manual tasks in achieving high master data quality. 

We leverage third-party data providers to drive automation in master data creation. We provide ways to predict master data attribute values from an application context, and we continue to use more artificial intelligence to support master data quality tasks. This will help to reduce the human effort in MDM and to avoid errors created by humans, and to avoid the effort it takes to correct these human errors.

This kind of automation will need an agile, rapidly-scaling MDM solution that can be extended fast, scales quickly, and easily integrates with new systems, new business processes and new data types. We think that in the long run, a modular approach to MDM might be more effective to meet this agility, more effective than a monolithic approach.

 

Strategy driver: modularization

 

Let’s now focus on federation/modularization. I have pointed out how a more modular application landscape requires a modular approach to MDM, and what other benefits in agility, speed, efficiency, and TCO we expect from that approach. Therefore, we provide this additional deployment option for MDG. This can be an option for companies that plan to balance central and decentral MDG processes instead of using a fully centrally managed MDG approach. This can for example be an alternative for very heterogeneous companies running very differently set up business processes and application systems in their landscape, and, for example, also multiple differently configured SAP S/4HANA systems. 

We believe in providing choices and non-disruptive journeys for our customers. This is why this additional deployment option is not only for new customers, but it can also be used by existing customers by adding to their current implementation and safeguarding their investments into MDG.

As of today, most companies are using one single MDG system to centrally manage the master data for the complete enterprise. Of course, they can continue to do so. But for some companies, one single monolithic MDM system for the entire organization presents certain challenges already today. And as we discussed, this will even increase in the future.

The new approach with a federated MDG landscape will allow these companies to go for a more modular approach. We believe that we provide companies with a faster time to value, with increased flexibility that enables a broader MDM scope, and with a lower Total-Cost-of-Ownership. Federation allows companies to execute master data management as centrally as possible, but also as locally as needed by the diverse parts of the organization. They can really govern the data at the place where it is best understood. You can find further details in the blog post about the benefits of federation.

SAP Master Data Governance product versions

SAP offers options for consuming SAP Master Data Governance across the cloud and on premise, so companies can decide which deployment approach works best for them.

  • On the left-hand side, you see SAP MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – which can also be deployed on premise. This version is typically used for multi-domain, centralized governance across complete enterprises. It has pre-delivered business content across various domains (for example, business partner, customer, supplier, material, financial data), and there are solution extensions from SAP Partners. This version comes with a very flexible framework to extend processes or create custom master data objects that even meet the very complex needs of the largest enterprises.
  • In the middle, there is SAP MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This is typically for organizations who are running SAP S/4HANA in the public cloud. This version currently focuses on business partner, customer, supplier, and product data. It is easy to consume and provides the processes for the very broad underlying S/4HANA data model.
  • And then, SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, shown on the right-hand side, is a software-as-a-service kick-start solution for the governance of core business partner data. 

We will continue to enhance all three versions of MDG. The strategy is for all versions of MDG to offer the same broad coverage of master data management processes across central governance, consolidation, data quality management and more. The two differences are: firstly, in the data model, which is either the very broad data model provided by SAP S/4HANA, or it is the core data model of SAP Business Technology Platform. The second difference is in how you can consume MDG, from a very easy to consume SaaS solution in the public cloud (on the right-hand side) to a robust private platform that supports even the most demanding customizations required by the world's largest companies (on the left-hand side).

Recent and Planned Innovations

I would now like to focus on some recent and planned innovations, and to explain how these relate to our product strategy. You may have already noticed the small graphic in the top right corner. It highlights which part of our strategy is addressed by each innovation.

Recent innovation: data provider integration

 

As mentioned earlier, digitization requires automation in MDM since lacking master data quality is a major barrier to innovation, to informed business decisions, and to the effective use of AI. We therefore invested into automation to help customers increase master data quality more efficiently with MDG. Automation in MDG always has two pillars. Pillar one is that leveraging trusted external data is much more efficient than typing it manually. And pillar two: we leverage technology – like AI – but also automated processes and rules-based automation.

The first recent innovation in this context is about automation with the help of trusted external data. We had already offered creation and enrichment of business partner data based on reference data from our partner Corporate Data Quality, who has access to more than 70 sources of trusted business partner data around the globe. Until recently, this was only possible in an interactive manner. We have now released a capability to add an automatic Reference Data Lookup step to MDG processes. This process step performs the search in the external data provider in the background and without user interaction. If this search finds a reference data record that is "good enough", it automatically takes over data, such as for example tax IDs and identification numbers and enriches the data in MDG. This further reduces the time users need to spend on master data management.

Recent innovation: generative AI for assisted changes and summary of changes

 Let's move on to two additional artificial intelligence scenarios that we have added recently.

  • The first one is “Summary of Changes”, which, for example, can help users who are approving master data changes. They get a natural language explanation of the requested changes.
  • The second scenario is “Assisted Change”. It allows users to change master data attributes using prompts in natural language. For example, people who are less trained or less experienced in master data management can communicate the intended change more easily to the system, and are then guided towards the right attributes in the data model that reflect their intended change.

We use Large Language Models for these capabilities, and customers can choose which LLM provider we should use. To achieve this, SAP has an abstraction layer, SAP’s AI foundation, which we leverage for our scenarios.

Planned innovation: integration with SAP Joule

Let's continue with the use of AI in MDG. The AI scenarios outlined earlier are directly embedded into MDG’s user interfaces. This way, they support users who already navigated into these UIs to work within MDG processes.

In addition, we have enabled MDG integration with SAP Joule, our generic copilot, which is always available for SAP users, regardless which process they are currently working in. Now, every business user can look up existing business partners in the system from their current work context. They just open the copilot in the side bar and start a chat in natural language, while SAP Joule guides them through a dialogue. If a user decides that changes to a business partner are needed, SAP Joule can take him or her to the right MDG user interface to carry out or trigger the changes.

Planned innovation: enhanced business partner governance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Now let's come to some planned innovations. Again, the graphic in the top-right corner shows how these relate to our vision. As you can see, this is related to modularization and the cloud.

Later this year, we plan to enhance the capabilities for the governance for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. On our way to leveraging cloud-ready mode in all MDG product versions, we will also use this mode in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to have a common development base for the MDG key capabilities. Cloud-ready mode in MDG was already introduced in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition in 2021 and in SAP MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in 2023.

This covers the processes for central governance – including inbound processing that triggers governance processes. It includes data provider integration with CDQ, as well as data quality management with validations and enrichment, and more.

For our customers on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this means they can introduce governance and control of the creation and change processes for business partner data, and they can increase effectiveness in the management of business partner data. As mentioned earlier, the focus now is on business partner data, further master data domains are planned for later – next in line is the product domain.

Planned innovation: processing business partner using SAP Joule

One more planned innovation that I would like to introduce also relates to AI. The recent innovation involving SAP Joule integration that I explained earlier was more about finding existing business partners or triggering changes by taking the user to the MDG user interface. We plan to expand this Joule dialog, so that the users can do even more without having to leave their work context.

With the planned innovation shown in the graphic above, business users can not only ask SAP Joule to search or display business partner data, but also use the AI copilot when it comes to changing or creating business partner data using natural language, without leaving their current tasks and without having to have any MDG expertise. They describe, in natural language, what master data changes they would like to initiate, and SAP Joule will guide them through a complete dialogue, and ultimately trigger the request for changes in the background. That way, every business user can seamlessly participate in master data processing, enhancing the effectiveness, speed, and automation of business partner governance. Instead of unstructured requests being sent to your master data team, who then has to enter these into the system, business users will now be supported by SAP Joule to provide structured input that only needs to be enriched and validated by the experts. And even those tasks can be largely automated by MDG through automatic derivations and validations. 

 Snapshot of MDG roadmap

 

Finally, here is an overview of all the innovations that we have planned for the next few years. All this information is also available in the SAP Roadmap Explorer. This slide reads from left to right, starting with recent innovations on the left-hand side and showing the planned innovations for 2025 and beyond. 

I know that this might look a bit of an eye chart, so I recommend taking a closer look at the Roadmap Explorer. For now, I have highlighted some of the most important planned innovations in a larger font:

Innovation highlights

When it comes to summarizing the innovation highlights, I have already mentioned the scenarios for AI that we already provided. We also provided innovations around data quality analytics in SAP Analytics Cloud, reference data processes and derivation scenarios.

With regards to the innovation highlights planned further up on the road, I already mentioned our plan to enable all relevant MDG processes for business partner data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Version. So far, we have offered that full functionality on SAP S/4HANA only for on-premise and private cloud deployments.

In addition, we intend to focus a lot on the product domain, which includes the cloud-ready mode architecture across all versions of MDG.

In parallel, we are working on further platform capabilities, like providing SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition on SAP BTP also as Platform-as-a-Service (currently, it is offered as Software-as-a-Service) and providing the cloud-ready mode option also for custom-defined objects.

In addition, there are all the other planned innovations shown in the background of the graphic that you can check out in detail in the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

SAP Master Data Governance in a nutshell

To summarize, SAP Master Data Governance is the one solution for your entire enterprise master data management program. It is an application that provides preconfigured, domain-specific master data governance. At the same time, it is an open platform to extend and build, in order to manage your own master data domains for all SAP and non-SAP landscapes.

MDG is also a key enabler that will help you get ready for your future business transformations. Many things are already available today, but we also discussed all the innovations on our roadmap for MDG that follow our main themes of automation, and federation to enable our customers’ digital transformation and their path to the cloud. Our roadmap for MDG will focus on unlocking these additional benefits for our customers, while SAP’s Business Suite vision continues to allow us to do more than just provide a toolbox for master data management.

To stay on track with what's going on in SAP MDG, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

Best,
Markus

entry.title: Re: Links to SAP demo recordings for HCM Business AI features
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec :  The Business AI for HR - Set up Guide has old content as Base AI and Premium AI calculation has changed after Sapphire. Navigational and Informational Capability pattern is Base AI whereas Transactional & Analytical Capability Pattern are Premium Joule Features. Also the Pricing & AI Units consumption are based on the No of Users and Per User Per Month Consumption.

entry.title: Re: How to display the fields vertically
entry.content: Latetly, tag "ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model" is being used really bad in all abap questions
